name: seed
context: Create and run Prisma database seed scripts to populate development and test data
info: Database seeding populates your database with initial or test data. Prisma seeds are TypeScript or JavaScript scripts that use the Prisma Client to insert records. The seed script is configured in `package.json` under `prisma.seed` (or `prisma.config.ts` for Prisma v7+) and executed with `npx prisma db seed`. Seeds run after `prisma migrate reset` to repopulate data. Use `upsert` instead of `create` to make seeds idempotent — safe to run multiple times. See [[prisma/client]] for query methods used in seeds and [[prisma/migrate]] for the reset workflow.

sections.title: Seed script setup
sections.body: Configure the seed command in `package.json` under `prisma.seed`. Use `tsx` or `ts-node` for TypeScript seeds, or `node` for JavaScript. The seed file is typically `prisma/seed.ts`. Install the runner as a dev dependency (e.g., `npm install tsx --save-dev`).

sections.title: Idempotent seeding
sections.body: Use `upsert` with `update: {}` to make seeds safe for repeated execution. This creates records on first run and skips them on subsequent runs. Never use `create` in seeds — it fails on duplicate unique fields (P2002). For bulk data, use `createMany` with `skipDuplicates: true`.

sections.title: Generating test data
sections.body: Use `@faker-js/faker` to generate realistic test data. Faker provides methods for emails, names, addresses, lorem text, dates, and more. Combine with loops and nested creates to populate complex data structures. Install with `npm install @faker-js/faker --save-dev`.
